hypothesis: Enhancing steric complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 regions by introducing bulky aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will increase selectivity margin by strengthening SSTR2 binding while reducing off-target affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is generating candidates with strong ddG but poor structural quality and limited selectivity gains. Tightening structural constraints and focusing mutations on non-pharmacophore positions will improve both structural quality and selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Reducing the clash tolerance will enforce better packing and reduce structural failures due to steric clashes, especially in the binding pocket.

To improve selectivity, we need to disrupt conserved pocket contacts in SSTR3/SSTR5 at non-pharmacophore positions. Focusing mutations at these positions will allow us to fine-tune interactions without affecting the conserved pharmacophore region (FWKT 7-10).
